w:r.w:t: You are absolutely right. If this module only spits out hostile goblins, it reduces a rich, narrative RPG into a shallow combat simulator.
An "Encounter" in a true TTRPG is simply a
. It is any moment the players must spend resources (Stamina, Focus, HP, Composure, or Items) to progress.
To support the massive depth of your 12-attribute system, the 36 Tactical Triads, and the Dual-Track Injury system, this module must act as a
. It needs to mathematically construct traps, puzzles, wandering merchants, and moral dilemmas just as flawlessly as it builds a monster.
Here is the developer-ready blueprint for the newly expanded Module 4.
🏭 S.A.G.A. BLUEPRINT 04: The Encounter & Obstacle Engine
A standalone Python microservice. The Game Master App sends it a location and a "Threat/Tension Level." This engine rolls on procedural tables to determine the
of encounter (Combat, Social, Hazard, Puzzle, Discovery, Dilemma) and outputs the strict mathematical framework required for the VTT and Rules Engine to run it.
Pydantic (Using Polymorphic models so one API endpoint can return vastly different JSON structures based on the encounter type).
│ ├── hazard_templates.json # Base logic for traps and environmental dangers
│ ├── puzzle_logic.json # Logic gates for locked doors, magical wards
│ └── npc_archetypes.json # Personalities, motives, and social stats
├── schemas.py # Pydantic schemas for the 6 Encounter Types
├── gen_social.py # Logic for merchants, quest-givers, factions
├── gen_hazard.py # Logic for traps, diseases, and weather survival
├── gen_puzzle.py # Logic for logical blockers and skill-checks
This is the core of the module. We define an Enum for the 6 types of friction, and a specific schema for each.
# Social HP. If players deplete this via Intimidation/Charm, the NPC yields.
# e.g., [{"choice": "Save the Merchant", "consequence": "Lose 2 Stamina, Gain Item"}, {"choice": "Let him die", "consequence": "Take 1 Mind Injury"}]
# GM can force a Trap, or leave blank for Random
4. Internal Logic & Code Execution (How to Code It)

The API receives the request. If forced_type is empty, it rolls a weighted d100 based on the biome. (e.g., A "Civilized City" node has a 60% chance for SOCIAL, 10% for COMBAT. A "Deep Dungeon" has a 50% chance for HAZARD, 40% COMBAT, 10% DISCOVERY).
If the faction culture has the trade: true toggle, spawn a Merchant NPC.
Calculate the NPC's composure_pool using the 6/6 stat math (Willpower + Logic + Awareness) from your rules.
Assign the Detection Triad: Awareness + Knowledge.
Assign the Survival Triad: Endurance + Fortitude.
If failed, assign the consequence directly to the Dual-Track Injury System (e.g., effect: "Apply 1 Minor Body Injury").
Because this API is polymorphic, here is what the Game Master App receives depending on what the Encounter Engine rolled.
"The path is choked with thick, pulsing green vines covered in translucent thorns."
Example Output B: The Social Obstacle
"A heavily armored guard from the Northern Horde steps into the road, spear raised."
Example Output C: The Dilemma (Hard Choice)
"You find a courier crushed under a fallen wagon. He is holding a sealed letter. He begs for your healing salve."
Why this design perfectly supports your vision:
By treating "Encounters" as a broader concept of "Friction", you ensure that characters built for
are just as valuable as characters built for physical combat.
can defeat the Overgrown Razor-Vines hazard, while a player with high
can engage in "Social Combat" to deplete the Border Guard's 18 Composure without drawing a sword.
Does this multi-faceted, heavily mechanical Encounter Engine fit the complete scope of your game? If so, we can move to
